summary: Planning Board simplified tabs-only layout tested successfully. All 5 tabs (All Projects, Requests, Materials, Labours, Suppliers) render correctly. No summary cards, no construction stages overview, no alert banners - simplified layout confirmed. CRUD operations work for Materials tab. Minor issue: Suppliers table shows duplicate rows due to React key warning.

ui_bugs.issue: Duplicate vendor rows displayed. React console shows 'Encountered two children with the same key' warning. The same vendors appear twice in the list (17 shown but visually ~34 rows).

5 Tabs Render.Requests (with badge): PASS - Badge shows count when pending requests exist

features_verified: 5 Tabs: All Projects (with red badge), Requests, Materials, Labours, Suppliers
All Projects tab with projects table and correct columns
Requests tab with Site Requests and Payment Requests sections
Materials tab with table, Add button, filter buttons (Active/Inactive/All)
Labours tab with contractors table and Add button
Suppliers tab with vendors table and Add button
Simplified layout confirmed - NO summary cards, NO stages overview, NO alert banners
action_items: FIX: Suppliers table shows duplicate vendor rows - React key issue. Check fetchVendors() or vendor data source for duplicate entries
critical_code_review_comments: Line 112-120 in PlanningBoard.jsx: fetchVendors() may be returning duplicate data or the vendor_id keys may not be unique causing React to render duplicates

test_credentials.Planning: Demo Access tab -> Planning button (Suresh Iyer - planning@constructionos.com)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Planning Board simplified to tabs-only layout working correctly. 5 tabs verified. Known issue: Suppliers tab shows duplicate vendor rows (React key warning). The 'Encountered two children with the same key' console warning is causing visual duplication in the vendors table.
rca_of_issue: Duplicate vendors issue likely caused by either: (1) API returning duplicate vendor data, or (2) Vendors being added to state twice during fetchVendors(), or (3) vendor_id not being unique across all vendors in the database
